title: Evangelion drum n bass tune
summary: []: Evangelion drum n bass tune             Quote Post by Jayfive » Tue Nov 29, 2011 5:46 pm Theres only so many revved up euro-trance remixes of 'Cruel Angels Thesis' a person can stand, so this is relatively refreshing: http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=oS3gqMsespc Lots of samples from the english dub (including Spike's spencers Shinji scream upon seeing eviscerated Unit 2 from EoE) over some thunderous d&b. Enjoy :&#

chunks.content:
Article: Evangelion drum n bass tune
[]: Evangelion drum n bass tune             Quote Post by Jayfive » Tue Nov 29, 2011 5:46 pm Theres only so many revved up euro-trance remixes of 'Cruel Angels Thesis' a person can stand, so this is relatively refreshing: http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=oS3gqMsespc Lots of samples from the english dub (including Spike's spencers Shinji scream upon seeing eviscerated Unit 2 from EoE) over some thunderous d&b. Enjoy :)

[]:             Quote Post by robersora » Thu Dec 08, 2011 3:07 pm This one's better: http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=esworHJ-rjM&feature=related

[]:             Quote Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Thu Dec 08, 2011 3:45 pm @Jayfive doesn't the The Eva Monkey have something similar archived on his site in the omake section?

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Thu Dec 08, 2011 4:42 pm There doesn't really seem to be a lot of, um... actual musical content in either of them. (

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Thu Dec 08, 2011 6:07 pm They are more tracks than compositions, let put it that way @Jayfive doesn't the The Eva Monkey have something similar archived on his site in the omake section?

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Thu Dec 08, 2011 6:22 pm But a "track" is a composition.

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Fri Dec 09, 2011 4:13 am I see it as a track being just some loops and samples wheras as a composition is phrases and verses and stuff that someone actually sat down and...well...composed. A lot of dance music, especially the harder and faster ends of it is just the result of people dicking around with FL Studio

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Fri Dec 09, 2011 10:24 am That's...all well and good I guess, but who would want to listen to it?
